description:
You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has just received a support ticket about recurring errors in an application that lives in “/home/user/app”.  
    Inside that directory you will already find:

    1. /home/user/app/VERSION  
       • A single-line file that holds the current semantic version (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH).  
    2. /home/user/app/logs/app.log  
       • A plain-text log that contains several timestamped log entries in the exact format  
         “[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] LEVEL: message”.  
       • Some of these lines are “ERROR” level events; they are what we have to summarise.  

    Your task consists of four subtasks that can be completed with a minimal sequence of shell commands (≈ 3–4 commands total), **without using sudo or editing any other part of the filesystem**:

    Sub-task 1 – bump the patch version  
        • Read the value in /home/user/app/VERSION (for example “X.Y.Z”).  
        • Increase ONLY the patch segment (Z → Z+1) while leaving major and minor untouched.  
        • Overwrite /home/user/app/VERSION with the new value followed by a single trailing newline.  
          Nothing else may appear in that file.

    Sub-task 2 – extract the error lines  
        • From /home/user/app/logs/app.log, select every line whose LEVEL is exactly “ERROR”.  
        • Preserve the lines exactly as they are (including timestamps and the word “ERROR”).  
        • Write those lines, in the same order they appeared in the source file, to  
          /home/user/app/logs/error_summary.log.  
        • The summary file must contain only those error lines and must end with a newline.

    Sub-task 3 – create / update the changelog  
        • Create the file /home/user/app/CHANGELOG.md if it does not exist.  
        • Prepend (i.e. insert at the very top of the file, above any existing content) the following
          section, replacing “NEW_VERSION” with the value written in Sub-task 1:

            ## [NEW_VERSION] - 2024-05-17

            - Unable to connect to database  
            - Connection timed out

          Formatting rules the automated test will check:
            a) The heading line starts with exactly two “#” symbols followed by one space.  
            b) The version must be wrapped in square brackets.  
            c) The fixed date string “2024-05-17” must follow “ - ” (space-dash-space).  
            d) There is one completely blank line between the heading and the first bullet.  
            e) Each bullet starts with “- ” (dash + space) and contains ONLY the message portion
               that followed “ERROR: ” in the original log.  
            f) End the section with a single trailing newline (i.e. the file ends with “\n”).

    Sub-task 4 – quick visual verification  
        • Display (e.g. with “cat” or any other simple command) the contents of:
            - /home/user/app/VERSION  
            - /home/user/app/logs/error_summary.log  
            - /home/user/app/CHANGELOG.md  
        • This console output is only for human inspection; the grading script will read the files.

    When you are done, the automated checker will verify:

        /home/user/app/VERSION            → contains the bumped semantic version followed by “\n”
        /home/user/app/logs/error_summary.log
                                           → contains ONLY the original ERROR lines, in order
        /home/user/app/CHANGELOG.md       → begins with the exact 7-line block shown above,
                                             with “NEW_VERSION” properly substituted

    You do not need to touch any other files or install anything.  A concise solution would
    normally require no more than four shell commands.
truth:
<!-- Initial state that exists before the agent starts -->
    File: /home/user/app/VERSION  
    Initial content (including trailing newline):
        2.5.6\n

    File: /home/user/app/logs/app.log  
    Initial content (including trailing newline on each line):
        [2024-05-15 10:12:34] INFO: Service started\n
        [2024-05-15 10:12:35] ERROR: Unable to connect to database\n
        [2024-05-15 10:12:36] WARN: Retrying connection\n
        [2024-05-15 10:12:37] ERROR: Connection timed out\n
        [2024-05-15 10:12:40] INFO: Shutdown complete\n

    <!-- Expected final state after the agent finishes -->

    1. /home/user/app/VERSION  
       Expected content:
           2.5.7\n

    2. /home/user/app/logs/error_summary.log  
       Expected content:
           [2024-05-15 10:12:35] ERROR: Unable to connect to database\n
           [2024-05-15 10:12:37] ERROR: Connection timed out\n

    3. /home/user/app/CHANGELOG.md  
       The file must BEGIN with exactly the following 7 lines
       (the checker will allow additional older entries to follow):

           ## [2.5.7] - 2024-05-17
           
           - Unable to connect to database
           - Connection timed out
           
       That is:  
       • Line-1: heading with new version and date  
       • Line-2: blank  
       • Line-3: first bullet  
       • Line-4: second bullet  
       • Line-5: blank (file ends with a newline after this blank line)

    Permissions:  
       All files and the logs directory are owned by the unprivileged user; they are writable.
